reasoning: Hamas agreeing to disarm by December 31, 2025 is extremely unlikely based on compelling evidence. Key reasons: (1) Senior Hamas official Mohammed Nazzal explicitly stated 'can't commit to disarm' in an October 17, 2025 interview (search_3_summary). (2) Hamas has consistently rejected disarmament proposals from April-October 2025, viewing it as a "red line" without reciprocal Israeli concessions (search_3_summary, search_1_summary). (3) With only ~50 days remaining until the deadline, Phase 2 negotiations (where disarmament is supposed to be addressed) haven't even begun, and Phase 1 hostage releases remain incomplete (search_1_summary, search_2_summary). (4) Hamas's fundamental identity as a resistance movement makes disarmament politically impossible without major concessions that haven't been offered. The timeline constraints alone make this virtually impossible, compounded by explicit official rejections from Hamas leadership.
